name: ATX-1 v2.0: AEGIS Threat Matrix
description: ATX-1 v2.0 — 9 tactics, 25 techniques for autonomous AI agent threat modeling.

techniques.comment: The agent executes a destructive or high-impact action based on instructions from a user who does not hold owner-level authority. The agent treats the instruction as valid because no stakeholder model exists to distinguish authority levels.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model) â€” No mechanism to verify that the instruction source holds authority for the requested action scope.

techniques.comment: The agent accepts an instruction as authoritative based on implicit signals â€” such as the instruction appearing in a retrieved document, email body, or shared context â€” rather than explicit delegation from a verified principal.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model), RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” Instructions and data share a channel; no mechanism to verify instruction provenance.

techniques.comment: The agent distributes content (emails, messages, notifications) to a large number of recipients based on an authority claim that has not been verified. The spoofed authority may appear in a prompt, forwarded message, or injected context.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model), RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” No principal verification; authority claims in content treated as valid.

techniques.comment: The agent autonomously expands the scope of a delegated task beyond what was explicitly requested, including via tool invocation. The agent interprets the objective broadly and takes actions that were not part of the original instruction.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” Agent cannot evaluate whether expanded scope is within delegated authority; no deliberation surface to assess scope boundaries.

techniques.comment: The agent performs bulk operations (mass edits, batch deletions, large-scale API calls) without validating that the scope of the operation matches the intent of the instruction, including via tool invocation.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” Agent cannot assess aggregate impact of bulk actions; no proportionality check against instruction intent.

techniques.comment: Harmful intent is decomposed across multiple delegated steps â€” including across multiple agents where no single agent has full visibility of the objective â€” such that no individual action appears unsafe.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” Agent cannot evaluate aggregate intent across delegation chains; no mechanism to detect composition attacks.

techniques.comment: The agent chains multiple tool calls or intermediate steps such that each step appears valid, but the aggregate effect bypasses policy or authorization constraints.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” Agent cannot evaluate aggregate policy impact of chained tool calls; no compositional policy enforcement.

techniques.comment: The agent selects an irreversible action (permanent deletion, irrecoverable modification) through direct or tool-mediated system interaction when a reversible alternative (archival, soft-delete, flagging) would satisfy the objective.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model) â€” Agent cannot evaluate action severity or compare alternatives against a proportionality standard.

techniques.comment: The agent initiates an action through direct or tool-mediated system interaction that triggers a chain of cascading changes across interconnected systems, where downstream effects were not anticipated or authorized.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model) â€” Agent cannot model downstream effects of actions in interconnected systems; no cascade impact assessment.

techniques.comment: The agent exports or discloses data from the current context (conversation, session, task) to an unauthorized recipient, whether through direct exfiltration, indirect request compliance, or tool-mediated data transfer.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model), RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” No authorization check on data recipients; instructions in content treated as authorized requests.

techniques.comment: The agent discloses information that persists across sessions or is stored in long-term memory, context, or configuration â€” exposing data from previous interactions to unauthorized parties in later sessions.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” No session-scoped data isolation; persistent data accessible across session boundaries without authorization checks.

techniques.comment: The agent transfers sensitive information across trust or policy domains through interactions with other agents, systems, or contexts that individually appear authorized.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” No data classification across domain boundaries; no cross-domain transfer authorization.

techniques.comment: The agent reports that a task has been completed successfully when the action either failed, was partially completed, or produced a different outcome than reported. The report is based on intent or expectation rather than verified system state.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model) â€” No model of the distinction between action initiation and action completion; no outcome verification protocol.

techniques.comment: The agent misattributes an action to a different principal, system, or process than the one that actually performed it. This corrupts the audit trail and undermines accountability.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model) â€” No mechanism to distinguish between action performers; attribution based on context rather than verified provenance.

techniques.comment: The agent suppresses, omits, or fails to surface an execution failure â€” whether caused by provider constraints, tool errors, or internal exceptions â€” resulting in a divergence between reported state and actual state.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model) â€” No model of constraint layers affecting the agent; failure signals not propagated to operator governance.

techniques.comment: The agent enters a recursive invocation loop â€” spawning sub-tasks, calling tools repeatedly, or triggering self-referential execution chains â€” consuming unbounded compute, token, and time resources.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” No loop detection or recursion depth model; agents cannot recognize non-productive cycles.

techniques.comment: The agent consumes external resources (API calls, network bandwidth, storage, third-party service quotas) without limits or lifecycle management, degrading availability for other systems and users.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model), RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” No model of resource boundaries; no lifecycle governance for external resource consumption.

techniques.comment: An agent claims or assumes the identity of another agent in a multi-agent system, gaining unauthorized access to resources, delegations, or trust relationships associated with the spoofed identity.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model) â€” No cryptographic identity verification between agents; identity claims based on self-assertion.

techniques.comment: An agent injects malicious tasks into a multi-agent delegation chain, exploiting trust transitivity to have other agents execute actions that the originating agent is not authorized to perform.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC1 (No Stakeholder Model), RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” No independent governance verification; shared governance infrastructure creates single points of compromise.

techniques.comment: An agent influences the behavioral patterns of other agents through shared context, conversation, or operational interaction, causing gradual drift from their governance-defined behavior toward unverified or unauthorized operational patterns.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” No mechanism to isolate agent behavior from peer influence; behavioral norms adopted from interaction rather than governance.

techniques.comment: The agent influences a group of agents to converge on a shared but unsafe or unauthorized objective, even when individual agents would not independently select that action.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC3 (No Private Deliberation Surface) â€” No mechanism to detect anomalous convergence in multi-agent decision-making; consensus accepted without independent verification.

techniques.comment: An agent writes malicious, misleading, or unauthorized data into persistent memory stores (long-term memory, knowledge bases, configuration caches), corrupting future interactions that rely on this stored context.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” No integrity verification for persistent memory writes; memory content treated as trusted regardless of provenance.

techniques.comment: An agent modifies its own governance constraints, policy state, or constitutional articles through direct manipulation or indirect means (tool use, self-prompting, context manipulation), bypassing normal amendment procedures and audit logging.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” Governance configuration stored in mutable locations; no integrity verification or tamper detection for policy state.

techniques.comment: The agent performs actions outside the boundaries of monitoring, logging, and audit systems â€” whether by using unmonitored tool pathways, operating in contexts where observability is limited, or exploiting gaps in the monitoring infrastructure.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC2 (No Self-Model) â€” No model of the observability boundaries affecting the agent; agent cannot distinguish monitored from unmonitored pathways.

techniques.comment: The agent obscures the traceability of its actions by manipulating log entries, altering audit trails, fragmenting actions across multiple contexts, or using indirect execution paths that make causal attribution difficult.

metadata.name: Root Cause
metadata.value: RC4 (Prompt Injection Is Structural) â€” Audit infrastructure accessible to the agent; no structural separation between action execution and audit recording.
